cataloging_subject.label: English Prose Treatises of Richard Rolle de Hampole
cataloging_subject.scope_note: The thirteen-part medieval devotional selection as revised and published in EETS Original Series 20 in 1921 and represented by CME00058. This is not one medieval work by one author, not Lincoln Cathedral MS 91 as a whole, not Perry's materially different ten-item 1866 first edition, not the complete 1921 printed volume with notes and glossary, and not the University of Michigan encoding or repository XML as digital objects.

catalog_summary.title: English Prose Treatises of Richard Rolle de Hampole
catalog_summary.author: Richard Rolle (attributed), Walter Hilton, and anonymous or uncertain authors
catalog_summary.date: Fourteenth-century works in late-fourteenth- and fifteenth-century witnesses; first edition 1866, revised thirteen-item reissue 1921
catalog_summary.region: England; principally Northern English witnesses, with a Midland opening supplied for the Mixed Life

titles.value: English Prose Treatises of Richard Rolle de Hampole
notes: This is the cataloged edition-selection title. It is not evidence that Rolle authored every component.

titles.value: English Prose Treatises of Richard Rolle de Hampole: Who Died A.D. 1349

scope.description: Full title of Perry's materially different 1866 first edition

date_statements.display: By 1349 if the traditional Rolle attributions are accepted; exact composition dates and parts of the canon remain uncertain
notes: The terminus follows Rolle's death and applies only where component authorship is genuine; it is not a date for the later manuscript copies or the edited selection.

date_statements.display: By Walter Hilton's death in 1396 for Of Angels' Song and the Epistle on the Mixed Life
Fourteenth century if the text is correctly identified as an excerpt from Walter Hilton's Scale of Perfection
No later than the c. 1390-c. 1425 Harley MS 1022 witness
notes: This dates the surviving copy, not necessarily composition.

date_statements.display: Perry's first ten-item edited selection was published in 1866
notes: The 1866 edition lacks revised items VI and XIII and combines revised items III-IV, so it is a prior version rather than the identical thirteen-item unit.

date_statements.display: Revised, expanded, reset, and reissued in 1921

form_statements.description: Eleven numbered items are prose, item II is prose ending in a two-line prayer, and item XIII consists of two verse prayers. Editorial verse quotations inside prose headnotes or tailnotes do not change the cataloged form of those component works.

content_parts.label: I. Of the Virtues of the Holy Name of Jesus
content_parts.summary: A devotional exposition of the name Jesus as salvation, sweetness, love, healing, and contemplative joy.
notes: Perry uses Lincoln as text and prints Harley variants in footnotes.

content_parts.label: II. A Tale of Hampole's Temptation
content_parts.summary: A first-person exemplum in which a female-appearing devil tempts the hermit and is defeated through prayer and the sign of the cross; the narrative closes with a two-line prayer.

content_parts.label: III. On Imperfect Contrition
content_parts.summary: An exemplum, attributed in the manuscript to Richard the Hermit retelling Caesarius, about a Paris canon whose confession and promised amendment lack true contrition.
notes: This and item IV were one numbered item III in the 1866 first edition.

content_parts.label: IV. A Story of Perfect Contrition
content_parts.summary: A companion exemplum about a penitent scholar whose written sins are miraculously erased before he can speak them, signifying forgiveness through true contrition.
notes: The 1921 revision promotes this second narrative to a new item IV; it was continuous with item III in 1866.

content_parts.label: V. The Bee and the Stork
content_parts.summary: A moral allegory using the industrious, grounded, clean-winged bee and the heavy stork to teach labor, humility, charity, and freedom from earthly attachment.

content_parts.label: VI. Of the Life of a Certain Enclosed Maiden for the Love of Christ
content_parts.summary: An exemplum, said to be retold from Heraclides by Richard the Hermit, about a maiden who lives enclosed in a sepulchre to avoid occasioning sin and devotes herself to prayer, work, and contemplation.
notes: This numbered item is new in the 1921 revised text and is absent from Perry's 1866 edition.

content_parts.label: VII. A Notable Treatise on the Ten Commandments
content_parts.summary: A concise vernacular exposition of the Decalogue addressing idolatry, witchcraft, oaths, Sabbath observance, obedience, violence, sexual conduct, theft, false witness, and covetousness.

content_parts.label: VIII. Of the Seven Gifts of the Holy Ghost
content_parts.summary: A short didactic account of counsel, understanding, wisdom, strength, piety, knowledge, and fear of God as guides to righteous and contemplative life.

content_parts.label: IX. Of Delight and Yearning for God
content_parts.summary: A mystical devotional tract on pure and stable delight in Jesus, restraint of sensual desire, disciplined thought, and spiritual rest in divine love.

content_parts.label: X. Of Angels' Song
content_parts.summary: Walter Hilton's warning that contemplative union and perceived heavenly song must arise through grace, virtue, humility, and discretion rather than forced imagination or bodily sensation.
notes: Perry's supplied heading is The Anehede of Godd with mannis saule and his edition historically ascribes the untitled manuscript text to Rolle; current specialist cataloging identifies it as Walter Hilton's Of Angels' Song.

content_parts.label: XI. Epistle on the Mixed Life
content_parts.summary: Walter Hilton's pastoral counsel on active, contemplative, and mixed forms of Christian life, teaching the addressee to combine prayer and meditation with obligations to household, dependents, and neighbors.

locators.value: opening supplied from the component beginning f. 57r; exact end folio unresolved

notes: The printed text is composite: Royal supplies the beginning through p. 27 line 29, and the imperfect Lincoln copy supplies the remainder. A separate unshelfmarked Cambridge copy is quoted for dialect comparison.

content_parts.label: XII. An Epistle of Salvation
content_parts.summary: A devotional prose epistle on original and personal sin, salvation through Christ's Passion, penitence, the saving name of Jesus, degrees of charity, and heavenly reward.
notes: Perry supplies the title The Virtue of Our Lord's Passion. The current Lincoln catalog uses An Epistle of Salvation; the specialist handlist identifies it as material from Walter Hilton's Scale of Perfection.

content_parts.label: XIII. Two Verse-Prayers to the Virgin Mary
content_parts.summary: Two short anonymous Middle English Marian prayers added in the 1921 revision from Harley MS 1022, f. 61v.
notes: The printed headnote says Harl. MS 1002; current British Library cataloging and DIMEV establish Harley MS 1022.
Neither prayer appears in Perry's 1866 first edition.

content_parts.label: Quene of parage
content_parts.summary: A six-line orison asking the Virgin, queen of peerage and restored paradise, to teach heavenly bliss, comfort the joyless, and intercede for entry into heaven.
notes: DIMEV describes two three-line monorhyming stanzas with medial rhyme and rhyme pattern aaa.

content_parts.label: Mary so milde
content_parts.summary: An eight-line prayer asking Mary, for love of her child, to hear wild petitioners, pour grace on them, build them in bliss, and shield them from sin.

responsibilities.display_name: Richard Rolle (attributed), Walter Hilton, and anonymous or uncertain authors

scope.description: Composite catalog display only; component-level responsibilities below carry the actual attribution evidence
Texts explicitly or contextually assigned to Richard the Hermit in Thornton, or defended as Rolle's by Perry

assertion.notes: Perry's historical defense of a broad Rolle canon is not treated as settled modern authorship. Component rubrics and current catalog descriptions vary in strength.
Current institutional and specialist handlists identify the untitled text as Of Angels' Song; Perry instead transmitted Sir Frederic Madden's attribution to Rolle.
The specialist handlist identifies the passage as from Hilton's Scale of Perfection, while the holding-repository catalog titles it An Epistle of Salvation without author attribution.

scope.description: The 1866 first edition and editorial selection retained and revised in 1921
Initial editorial grouping and ordering of the manuscript extracts
The revised and expanded text printed in 1921
The revised glossary and Notes of the immediate 1921 volume; those paratexts are not encoded in CME00058

agents.name: Richard Rolle
notes: The edition title is not used as evidence for unscoped sole authorship.

scope.description: Rubrics, biblical quotations, prayers, formulae, and the first Marian prayer's Latin closer

language_statements.label: English

scope.description: Modern editorial titles, headnotes, marginal explanations, footnotes, and title-page matter retained by the encoding

notes: Editorial English is not the language of the underlying medieval works.
The selected works and their witnesses are English, but precise places of composition are not assigned from copy location or dialect alone.

scope.description: The dominant Lincoln Thornton and Harley readings, including the Lincoln remainder of Mixed Life

notes: This describes witness language, not necessarily the original composition region of every work.

scope.description: Royal MS 17 C XVIII opening supplied through printed p. 27 line 29

notes: Perry attributes the Midland diagnosis to Morris's verbal-plural test; the current BL catalog does not independently localize Royal MS 17 C XVIII.

scope.description: Publication place of the 1866 and 1921 edited selections

subject_statements.label: The name of Jesus, divine love, and contemplative joy
Commandments, virtues, vices, and gifts of the Holy Spirit
Active, contemplative, and mixed forms of Christian life
Christ's Passion, sin, salvation, charity, and heavenly reward
Marian devotion, intercession, grace, and heavenly bliss

summaries.value: A revised editorial selection of thirteen short Middle English devotional texts and prayers associated historically with Richard Rolle. It moves from the name and love of Jesus through temptation, contrition, moral exempla, commandments, gifts of the Holy Spirit, contemplative experience, Walter Hilton's teaching on angels' song and the mixed life, salvation through Christ's Passion, and two anonymous Marian prayers.

I Of the Virtues of the Holy Name; II Hampole's Temptation; III-IV imperfect and perfect contrition; V The Bee and the Stork; VI the enclosed maiden; VII Ten Commandments; VIII Seven Gifts of the Holy Ghost; IX delight and yearning for God; X Of Angels' Song; XI Epistle on the Mixed Life; XII An Epistle of Salvation; XIII Quene of parage and Mary so milde.

The record follows the 1921 revised thirteen-item selection encoded by CME00058. It excludes the rest of each physical manuscript, the 1866 preface and Officium, the 1921 prefatory note, Notes, Glossary and Index, advertisements, and other unencoded bound matter, while the lineage manifest records those physical and editorial layers separately.

extent.description: Thirteen top-level numbered sections containing fourteen component items because XIII contains two separate prayers. Items I and III-XII are prose; item II is prose ending in a two-line verse prayer; the two item-XIII prayers total 14 lines in four LG elements. The repository XML has 23 L elements overall: 16 intrinsic lines and seven lines inside editorial quotations or tailnotes in items X and XII. It also has 2 DIV1, 13 DIV2, 9 DIV3, 37 HEAD, 49 PB, 416 MILESTONE, 357 NOTE, 51 P, 1,576 HI, and 86 ABBR elements.

related_works.title: English Prose Treatises of Richard Rolle de Hampole (1866 ten-item selection)
notes: The 1921 unit is an expanded and renumbered revision, not an identical reprint.

related_works.title: The Scale of Perfection
notes: The specialist handlist identifies revised item XII, An Epistle of Salvation, as material from Walter Hilton's Scale; the exact excerpt relationship requires confirmation in a modern critical edition.

evidence.citation: General Corpus pinned CME source XML, CME00058.xml.
evidence.summary: Primary evidence for the encoded title, thirteen-part sequence, component headings and wording, printed-page milestones, manuscript-locus notes, languages, themes, forms, and editorial structure.

evidence.citation: Namespace-free XPath structural inspection of the pinned CME00058 XML.
evidence.summary: Counts 2 DIV1, 13 DIV2, 9 DIV3, 37 HEAD, 49 PB, 416 MILESTONE, 357 NOTE, 51 P, 23 L, 4 LG, 1,576 HI, and 86 ABBR elements. Ancestor inspection assigns 14 L to the two item-XIII prayers, two to item II's closing prayer, and seven to editorial quotations or tailnotes in items X and XII.

evidence.citation: University of Michigan Library Digital Collections, CME00058 item information and contents.
evidence.summary: Exact provider evidence for the title, 1921 source edition, stable identifier, and structured contents I-XIII.

evidence.citation: Internet Archive, complete Boston College copy of English Prose Treatises of Richard Rolle de Hampole (1921).
evidence.locator: Title PDF page 9; text pp. 1-48 at PDF pages 13-60
evidence.summary: Primary edition evidence for title, editorial responsibility, thirteen-item numbering, component titles, printed pages, summaries, mixed form, and revised-edition boundaries.

evidence.citation: Prefatory Note to English Prose Treatises of Richard Rolle de Hampole (1921).
evidence.locator: PDF page 11, unnumbered Prefatory Note dated 28 November 1921
evidence.summary: Primary evidence that Furnivall prepared the revised text and Day revised the glossary and added the Notes, and that the 1921 publication is a reissue with changed prefatory matter.

evidence.citation: Mabel Day's Notes to English Prose Treatises of Richard Rolle de Hampole (1921).
evidence.summary: Primary evidence for Day's editorial responsibility and cited witnesses. The p. 13 entry explicitly names Dd. v. 64, while the contiguous p. 17-20 entries abbreviate the same Cambridge source as MS. Camb.; this supports a high-confidence contextual inference but is not a folio-level verification.

evidence.citation: Wikimedia Commons and Internet Archive, complete copy of Perry's English Prose Treatises (1866).
evidence.locator: Title PDF page 5; text pp. 1-45 at PDF pages 37-81
evidence.summary: Primary evidence for Perry's original ten-item selection, its full title and 1866 date, and the exact structural differences from the expanded thirteen-item 1921 unit.

evidence.citation: George G. Perry, Preface to English Prose Treatises of Richard Rolle de Hampole (1866).
evidence.locator: Preface pp. v-vii within PDF pages 7-35
evidence.summary: Primary historical evidence for Perry's attribution arguments, Lincoln and Harley source descriptions, the composite Royal/Lincoln Mixed Life, and the unidentified Cambridge comparison witness.

evidence.citation: University of Lincoln Special Collections catalog, Lincoln Cathedral MS 91.
evidence.summary: Holding-repository evidence for date, provenance, the exact sequence and loci of the relevant Lincoln texts, component titles, and current attribution labels.

evidence.citation: Database of Middle English Romance, Lincoln Cathedral Library MS 91 handlist.
evidence.summary: Specialist evidence for North Yorkshire association and modern identifications of Of Angels' Song and Mixed Life as Walter Hilton's and item XII as from Hilton's Scale of Perfection.

evidence.citation: British Library Archives and Manuscripts Catalogue, Harley MS 1022.
evidence.summary: Exact repository evidence for the c. 1390-c. 1425 Northern English witness, the two Marian prayers at f. 61v, and Rolle's Oleum effusum at ff. 62r-64r.

evidence.citation: British Library Archives and Manuscripts Catalogue, Royal MS 17 C XVIII.
evidence.summary: Exact repository evidence for the fifteenth-century witness and Walter Hilton's incomplete Epistle on the Mixed Life beginning at f. 57 before the following item at f. 65.

evidence.citation: Digital Index of Middle English Verse version 1.0, records 4432 and 3437.
evidence.summary: Versioned scholarly evidence for the prayers' titles, subjects, form, lineation, first and last lines, anonymity, and exact Harley MS 1022 f. 61v loci.

open_questions.question: Which of revised items I-IX belong in the modern Richard Rolle canon, and what confidence should attach to Walter Hilton's authorship of item X and the Hilton source identification for item XII?
next_steps: Consult modern critical editions and authorial-canon studies for each component rather than relying on Perry's 1866 defense.
Preserve manuscript rubrics, Perry's attributions, and current catalog labels as separate assertions.
Keep the catalog summary mixed and scoped until the components are reviewed individually.

open_questions.question: What exact passage of Walter Hilton's Scale of Perfection corresponds to An Epistle of Salvation at Lincoln MS 91, ff. 229v-230v, and is the manuscript unit an excerpt, adaptation, or separately transmitted epistle?
next_steps: Collate the Lincoln incipit and explicit against a modern critical edition of the Scale.
Check modern Hilton manuscript scholarship for the title An Epistle of Salvation.
Revise the related-work relation and authorship confidence only after the textual boundary is verified.

open_questions.question: Can individual component compositions be dated or localized more precisely without conflating authorial origin, witness dialect, manuscript production, and edition publication?
next_steps: Use component critical editions and Linguistic Atlas profiles for each exact witness passage.
Retain the Royal opening's historical Midland diagnosis separately from the dominant Northern witness language.
Do not assign North Yorkshire as the composition place of every selected work merely because Thornton copied them there.

open_questions.question: What is the shelfmark of Perry's unnamed Cambridge Mixed Life witness, and can direct manuscript inspection confirm that Day's contiguous abbreviated 'MS. Camb.' variants are all CUL Dd.5.64?
next_steps: Ask Cambridge University Library to identify Perry's quoted comparison sentence.
Inspect Dd.5.64 at the cited passages or obtain a complete-object reproduction.
Treat the contiguous Notes abbreviations as a high-confidence contextual inference, not as a demonstrated identity with the separate unshelfmarked Mixed Life witness.

notes: The title-page phrase 'of Richard Rolle' is retained as the edition-selection title but is not normalized into unscoped sole authorship.
Composition dates, copy dates, edition dates, and digital-encoding dates are kept separate.
The part inventory follows the 1921 revised numbering represented by CME00058, not the ten-item numbering of the 1866 first edition.
